subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – A Triumph of Surgery
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Tricki was a small pet dog who had become excessively fat and inactive over time. He spent most of his day lying on cushions and showed no interest in running or playing. His eyes looked dull, and his body appeared bloated. Seeing his unhealthy condition, Mrs Pumphrey grew anxious and believed that Tricki was suffering from a serious illness that required immediate attention.
questions: Who was Tricki?
What physical condition had Tricki developed?
How did Tricki spend most of his day?
How did Tricki’s appearance reflect his health?
Who became anxious about Tricki’s condition?

passages.paragraph: Mrs Pumphrey loved Tricki deeply and treated him like a child. She believed that feeding him rich food would keep him strong and happy. As a result, she gave him cream cakes, chocolates, and sweet dishes regularly. However, this excessive feeding weakened Tricki’s body instead of improving his health.
questions: How did Mrs Pumphrey treat Tricki?
What belief did she have about feeding Tricki?
What types of food were given to Tricki?
How often was Tricki fed rich food?
What effect did excessive feeding have on Tricki?

passages.paragraph: The veterinary surgeon examined Tricki carefully and quickly understood the real cause of his illness. Tricki was not suffering from any disease; instead, his poor health was due to overfeeding and lack of physical exercise. The vet advised Mrs Pumphrey to cut down on food and ensure that Tricki exercised regularly.
questions: Who examined Tricki?
Did Tricki suffer from a disease?
What was the real cause of Tricki’s illness?
What advice did the vet give about food?
What suggestion was made regarding exercise?

passages.paragraph: Mrs Pumphrey found it extremely difficult to follow the vet’s advice. Her affection for Tricki made her continue pampering him despite warnings. As days passed, Tricki’s condition worsened instead of improving. Finally, the vet decided that hospitalisation was necessary to strictly control Tricki’s daily routine.
questions: Why did Mrs Pumphrey find it difficult to follow the advice?
What did she continue to do despite the warnings?
What happened to Tricki’s condition over time?
Who decided on hospitalisation?
Why was hospitalisation considered necessary?

passages.paragraph: At the veterinary surgery, Tricki was placed under strict supervision. For the first two days, he was given no food so that his digestive system could recover. This controlled routine allowed his body to rest and heal naturally. Soon, Tricki began showing signs of improvement.
questions: Where was Tricki taken for treatment?
What was done during the first two days?
Why was Tricki not given food initially?
What benefit did the controlled routine provide?
What change was noticed in Tricki afterward?

passages.paragraph: After a few days at the surgery, Tricki started mixing with other dogs. He began running and playing with them regularly. This physical activity helped him regain strength and energy. Gradually, Tricki transformed into an active and cheerful dog once again.
questions: What did Tricki start doing after a few days?
With whom did Tricki interact?
How did physical activity help Tricki?
What changes were seen in Tricki’s strength?
What did Tricki become gradually?

passages.paragraph: Mrs Pumphrey remained anxious during Tricki’s stay at the surgery. Out of concern, she sent eggs, wine, and brandy to the vet. She believed that Tricki was undergoing serious medical treatment and that these items would help him recover faster and regain strength.
questions: How did Mrs Pumphrey feel during Tricki’s stay?
What items did she send to the vet?
Why did she send these items?
What did she believe about Tricki’s treatment?
What result did she expect from sending the items?

passages.paragraph: In reality, the vet did not use any medicines or perform surgery on Tricki. Instead, he relied on discipline, routine, and regular exercise. This simple method proved highly effective and restored Tricki’s health without any medical intervention.
questions: Did the vet use medicines?
Was surgery performed on Tricki?
What method did the vet rely on?
How effective was this method?
What was the final result of the treatment?

passages.paragraph: When Tricki finally returned home, he looked healthy, active, and energetic. Mrs Pumphrey was overjoyed to see her pet in such good condition. She thanked the vet repeatedly and believed that advanced medical treatment had saved Tricki’s life.
questions: How did Tricki look when he returned home?
Who was overjoyed at Tricki’s return?
Whom did Mrs Pumphrey thank?
What did she believe had saved Tricki?
Why was Mrs Pumphrey grateful?

passages.paragraph: The story clearly shows that excessive love without discipline can be harmful. True care requires moderation, responsibility, and understanding. Using humour and irony, the author conveys this important message in a simple and effective way that readers can easily relate to real life.
questions: What does the story clearly show?
What kind of love is described as harmful?
What does true care require?
Which literary techniques are used by the author?
Why is the message easy for readers to relate to?
